heading: Agreements for operation of school loan funds
label: 292q Agreements for operation of school loan funds
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 721 of act , was classified to prior to the general revision of this subchapter by .July 1, 1944section 293a of this titlePub. L. 102–408

children.heading: Fund agreements
children.label: a Fund agreements
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to enter into an agreement for the establishment and operation of a student loan fund in accordance with this subpart with any public or other nonprofit school of medicine, osteopathic medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, podiatric medicine, optometry, or veterinary medicine.
provide for establishment of a student loan fund by the school;

children.label: A
children.text: the Federal capital contributions to the fund;

children.label: B
children.text: an amount equal to not less than one-ninth of such Federal capital contributions, contributed by such institution;

children.label: C
children.text: collections of principal and interest on loans made from the fund;

children.label: D
children.text: collections pursuant to ; andsection 292r(j) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: any other earnings of the fund;
provide that the fund shall be used only for loans to students of the school in accordance with the agreement and for costs of collection of such loans and interest thereon;

provide that loans may be made from such funds only to students pursuing a full-time course of study at the school leading to a degree of doctor of medicine, doctor of dentistry or an equivalent degree, doctor of osteopathy, bachelor of science in pharmacy or an equivalent degree, doctor of pharmacy or an equivalent degree, doctor of podiatric medicine or an equivalent degree, doctor of optometry or an equivalent degree, or doctor of veterinary medicine or an equivalent degree;

provide that the school shall advise, in writing, each applicant for a loan from the student loan fund of the provisions of under which outstanding loans from the student loan fund may be paid (in whole or in part) by the Secretary; andsection 292r of this title
contain such other provisions as are necessary to protect the financial interests of the United States.

children.heading: Failure of school to collect loans
children.label: c Failure of school to collect loans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any standard established by the Secretary by regulation for the collection by schools of medicine, osteopathic medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, podiatric medicine, optometry, or veterinary medicine of loans made pursuant to loan agreements under this subpart shall provide that the failure of any such school to collect such loans shall be measured in accordance with this subsection. This subsection may not be construed to require such schools to reimburse the student loan fund under this subpart for loans that became uncollectible prior to August 1985 or to penalize such schools with respect to such loans.

children.heading: Extent of failure
children.label: 2 Extent of failure
children.text: The measurement of a school’s failure to collect loans made under this subpart shall be the ratio (stated as a percentage) that the defaulted principal amount outstanding of such school bears to the matured loans of such school.

children.label: i
children.text: make an installment payment when due; or

children.label: ii
children.text: comply with any other term of the promissory note for such loan,

children.label: i
children.text: repayable monthly and in default for at least 120 days; and

children.label: ii
children.text: repayable less frequently than monthly and in default for at least 180 days;

children.label: C
children.text: The term “grace period” means the period of one year beginning on the date on which the borrower ceases to pursue a full-time course of study at a school of medicine, osteopathic medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, podiatric medicine, optometry, or veterinary medicine; and

children.label: i
children.text: enrolled in a full-time course of study at such school; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in their grace period.
